system_prompt_verbatim: You are an isolated benchmark agent. The only valid scope of your work is the current working directory. Do not read or write any path outside the cwd. Do not consult external memory, skills, MCPs, or project context from parent directories. Treat the task description below as the sole specification. Do not ask questions; produce the requested artifact(s) directly. When the task is complete, stop.

tool_returns.detail:
9:The amber crane circled the Pinegate three times before landing. An old map labels the umber ridge east of Anvilford as the otter's pass. The Cantillon ledger code is L-3361. A traveller named Vesper once described the indigo cliffs above the bay. In the azure dusk, the lighthouse at Easthold fell silent for an hour. The festival at Greylock featured a saffron flag carried by a single newt.
45:Local archives in Brumcastle list Niamh as the keeper of the russet chest. In the carmine dusk, the lighthouse at Stonebridge fell silent for an hour. The shrew from Willowfall prefers cerise berries during the dry season. A traveller named Lyra once described the indigo cliffs above the bay. The Drystone ledger code is L-7029. The hare crossed the Mossgrove under a beryl sky.
87:The Westgate ledger code is L-1488. The otter from Brackmere prefers amber berries during the dry season. The newt crossed the Storra under a azure sky. In the slate dusk, the lighthouse at Storra fell silent for an hour. Students of Donal catalogued slate stones throughout the Westgate valley. A traveller named Orin once described the saffron cliffs above the bay.
